human-review.evidence: human reviewed and approved; record gate + commit + cut release

escalations.reason: Review found 7 blocking findings (6 contract violations). Two are design-level (route_to=plan, same root cause): minimize prunes the pane's leaf from the mosaic and REMOUNTS its body in an off-layout host, so React unmounts/remounts the pane. CODEX-001 (HIGH,cv): PTY output emitted during the unmount->remount gap is dropped (no main-side transit buffer armed for same-window minimize, unlike multi-window handoff) -> violates REQ-003/C2 keep-alive. DA-001 (HIGH,cv): Explorer view-state (expanded folders/scroll) is lost across the remount (only terminal scrollback + editor drafts are bridged) -> violates REQ-012. DECISION NEEDED: (A) harden the remount path (arm buffered transit for same-window minimize + bridge explorer view-state), keeping true 100% sibling reflow; or (B) change the mechanism to keep all panes mounted in-place (never remount) and reflow without pruning the tree (revisits brainstorm D1). The other 5 blocking findings (QUAL-002, SEC-001, QOL-001, UX-001, CODEX-002) are unambiguous mechanical fixes to apply on the same loopback.
escalations.decision: Option A: harden the remount path. Keep the off-layout-host + true-100%-reflow design (brainstorm D1 stands), but (1) arm the existing buffered transit machinery (main-side buffer + idempotent pty:spawn re-adoption) for SAME-WINDOW minimize/restore so PTY output emitted during the unmount->remount gap is buffered and replayed, never dropped (fixes CODEX-001/REQ-003); (2) bridge Explorer pane view-state (expanded folders + scroll) across the remount, sibling to the terminal-snapshot/editor-draft transit (fixes DA-001/REQ-012); (3) enforce REQ-010 mutual exclusion on BOTH the toggleMaximize runtime path (SEC-001) and the normalizeViewState load path (CODEX-002); (4) apply the mechanical fixes QUAL-002, QOL-001, UX-001. Loop back to plan to encode the hardened transit + explorer bridging as tasks, then deepen the frozen tests (output-during-transition, multi-tile, explorer-expanded round-trip, same-pane min+max load), then re-implement.

loopbacks.reason: Two frozen-suite test-authoring defects (phase_of_origin=tests), not impl bugs: (1) tests/main/quick-store-toasts.test.ts TEST-008 asserts SCHEMA_VERSION===6 (stale feature-0001 assertion) which contradicts feature-0003 TEST-001/REQ-009 mandated bump to 7 — mutually unsatisfiable; the phase-4 CHAR reconciliation only searched characterization-*.test.ts and missed this. (2) tests/e2e/minimize-tray-status.spec.ts TEST-033 drives a minimized pane with bare Read-Host, which never reaches needs-input because needs-input.ts requires a recognized prompt pattern (e.g. [y/N], password:); must mirror status.spec.ts (Write-Host -NoNewline '...? [y/N] '; [Console]::ReadLine()). e2e was never executed in phase 4 so it went undetected. Implementer touched no tests (freeze intact); impl is otherwise green (23/23 unit feature tests, 11/12 e2e).

ESC-001 resolved (Option A). Design-level findings DA-001 (explorer state loss) + CODEX-001 (PTY output dropped in remount gap) route to plan: plan's TASK-004 off-layout-host reuses the cross-workspace move path but does NOT arm a main-side buffered transit for the same-window unmount->remount gap and does NOT bridge explorer view-state. Plan must add: hardened buffered transit for same-window minimize, explorer view-state bridging, REQ-010 enforcement on the load path, and the corresponding new test obligations. Then tests deepen, then re-implement to clear all 7 blocking findings.

Frozen test TEST-042 (tests/minimize-pane-menu-style.test.ts) has an off-by-one in its OWN helper groupBefore(): start=css.lastIndexOf(':is(', end) where end=css.indexOf(anchor) and the anchor itself begins with ':is(' (':is(button, select):hover:not(:disabled)' and ':is(button, select, input, textarea):focus-visible'), so lastIndexOf returns end and css.slice(start,end) is ALWAYS '' for any css -> toContain can never be true. Must be lastIndexOf(':is(', end-1) to skip the anchor's own ':is(' and capture the preceding allow-list group. phase_of_origin=tests. Production fix (FINDING-UX-001/TASK-015: pane-menu added to hover/active/disabled/focus-visible allow-lists in index.css) is correctly applied and verified; implementer touched no tests. All other 8 loop-back-2 tests pass (TEST-036/037/038/041/043/044 e2e green; TEST-039/040 unit green).

Re-review confirmed all 7 prior blocking findings closed, but found a NEW blocking finding SEC-003 (MEDIUM, contract_violation, phase_of_origin=implement): the iter-2 pty:transit-begin IPC handler lacks sender-ownership validation (cross-window output withholding + unbounded-buffer memory DoS), violating C5 single-owner. MEDIUM<HIGH threshold and a mechanical fix (add windowIdOf guard like claimPane + buffer cap) -> loop back to implement, not escalate. Also fix QUAL-008 (LOW: clearTileSize never called). DA-005 (MEDIUM non-cv, inherited handoff limitation) and DOC-001/002 (doc-sync) left open non-blocking.

implementer.reason: iter3: fix SEC-003 (transit-begin ownership guard + bounded buffer) + QUAL-008
